Topic 6: Part 1 (last week's recap: same deals & disagreements)

[180:53]U.S. and Iran have finally come to an agreement.

[181:06]As you know, the MOU concerns a few major issues, and I laid them out very systematically

[181:13]last week.

[181:14]I like to think of the MOU as formalizing the ceasefire, which already existed.

[181:22]Point number one is it ends the war.

[181:24]We had fighting between the US, the Gulf countries in Israel, and Iran over the Persian Gulf.

[181:31]So the first thing the MOU does is it ends the war.

[181:34]Second thing it does is it opens up the Strait of Hormuz on both sides.

[189:34]about it as well.

[193:19]What's more, the Iranians maintain that they are going to now manage the straight.

[193:25]And as soon as the Iranian delegation left Switzerland this weekend, they went right

[193:29]to Oman, where pursuant to the MOU, Iran and Oman will now work out the terms of how they

[193:37]will jointly govern the straight.

[193:45]of managing the straight.

[193:52]of it, they're going to be charging fees.

[194:05]are formally and officially going to manage

[194:07]and control the straight

[194:09]and they're gonna be charging money to use it.

[194:30]because this is a very complicated question.

[194:36]These other things can be spun in the media.

[194:46]The White House, I thought the stream went down.

[194:50]I saw people going a little nuts in the live chat.

[194:52]The White House can simply lie about Iran controlling the straight or charging a fee.

[195:13]satisfy these conditions so that the US can leave the conflict. We talked about even some

[195:19]of the other issues like a nuclear, like the nuclear negotiations. We could extend that

[195:42]it's going to be an issue.

[196:35]it a buffer zone between Lebanon and Israel.

[197:17]they will just hold onto the territory forever.

[197:31]they're gonna cross over the border

[203:22]US is saying it's open, it's open, but the shipping has not returned to the pre-war levels.

[204:07]We're not gonna stop bombing Lebanon.

[204:26]to uphold our end of the bargain.

[204:28]and even advanced to the second phase of the MOU,

[204:38]which is that the US made it official.

[204:45]for US sanctions against Iranian oil.

[204:48]This was also part of the MOU.

[204:50]There were a lot of give-mys in the MOU for Iran.

[204:54]And it was talked about a lot last week,

[204:57]There's a $300 billion reconstruction fund allocated among private sector investors from

[205:04]East Asia and the Middle East, but they don't get that until the completion of the deal.

[205:09]There was talk about $100 to $200 billion in frozen Iranian assets that again upon completion

[205:16]of the deal would be unfrozen and returned to Iran.

[205:19]But the biggest concession is the one that they get upfront at the very beginning before

[205:25]they do anything, and that is their ability to sell their oil.

[205:37]oil.

[205:37]Iran is still technically been able to sell oil but they have to do it clandestinely on

[205:42]the black market.

[205:43]They built up a shadow fleet of tankers and they only sell them to a couple of countries

[205:48]like China and India, countries that are willing to take on the risk of doing business with

[205:53]Iran and they risk incurring the wrath of the United States.

[205:58]And because Iran can only sell to a couple of countries, they can't even sell their

[206:03]oil at market price and they can't sell all of it.

[206:07]So they don't make as much money from their oil because there isn't competition to buy it

[206:12]And there's this premium because they have to go on the black market effectively to sell it

[206:17]Because they have to get around the sanctions

[206:19]So the biggest the biggest thing the biggest concession they're getting up front is that the United States as part of the MOU

[206:26]Said that we would no longer sanction their oil

[206:30]The Treasury Department would give them these waivers and so then immediately they're able to sell their oil. Now

[206:37]why did we give that to them up front? Well one and

[206:41]this may be obvious, maybe it's less obvious. The reason we gave this to Iran up front was effectively as a bribe

[206:49]to keep them in the negotiations.

[206:52]Diplomacy was about to fall apart around this time last week.

[206:56]Because although the U.S. and Iran were on the precipice of signing the deal, Israel bombed Beirut and Iran vowed to retaliate against Israel with a missile strike.

[207:06]And it was rumored and reported that the administration bribed Iran not to retaliate and stay inside the diplomatic process and make the deal by offering them these waivers on the sanctions immediately.

[207:22]So we gave them this as a huge concession,

[207:25]basically just to bribe them to make a deal at all.

[207:28]The other reason we did this up front

[207:30]is because this is going to relieve pressure on oil prices.

[207:34]If we could get Iran's oil out to the market,

[207:37]then the overall supply of energy increases

[207:40]and that eases.

[207:42]The upward pressure on prices because of the closure of the straight and all the time

[207:48]It's going to take to adjust to get all the soil out of the straight and to its customers

[207:53]So we did it to bribe Iran. We also did it because we need it

[207:57]We needed to keep prices stable because now our strategic petroleum reserve is running out

[208:03]So anyway, so this is a big thing that Iran is getting up front. It's a sweetener. Also. We need it realistically more than they do

Topic 9: Part 4 (their new oil trade & complete concession)

[208:11]But then today it was announced not only does Iran get to sell their oil without sanctions,

[208:17]but it was also announced they get to sell their oil in dollars.

[208:36]to settle in their account immediately.

[208:38]They're going to start making revenue and profit from the oil immediately because dollars

[208:47]They badly need US dollars. They've been cut off from the global financial system. So this is not

[208:53]only a boon to them in the sense that they get the revenue, but they also get the currency

[208:59]to ease some of their currency and monetary problems as well.

[213:17]where we are constantly chasing them,

[213:19]constantly begging them to stay at the table,

Topic 10: Part 5 (nuclear inspection & similarities)

[218:27]Vance says, well, the reason we're letting them sell the oil and dollars is because they're

[218:33]going to let nuclear inspectors into their country.

[218:38]But here's the thing about this.

[218:40]Iran already has allowed IAEA inspectors back into the country.

[218:45]The question is where?

[218:48]Where are they letting the inspectors?

[218:50]Up until this point, Iran is letting the inspectors into Bushir.

[218:55]Bushir is the city on Iran's coast where they have their nuclear power plants.

[219:00]But Iran is not lending the inspectors to Fordo, Natanz, and Esfahan, which is where

[219:08]the highly enriched uranium is.

[219:10]That's where the centrifuges are.

[219:12]That's where the research is being conducted.

[219:14]So the inspectors, in other words, they're looking at the peaceful power plant.

[219:19]They're not looking at where the bomb would be made.

[219:23]And why do we want the inspectors to go there?

[219:26]as opposed to Bushir. We want the inspectors to go to Natanz, Esfahan and Fordo because we want to

[219:33]assess the damage that was done by Midnight Hammer and by the war this year. We want to see, does

[219:40]Iran still have the capability to enrich? What is their level of access to this infrastructure?

[219:47]How degraded is the highly enriched uranium and where is it?

[219:50]So it's a question of where the inspectors will be allowed and Iran is insistent.

[219:56]They will not let the inspectors see those three sites.

[220:01]Now Vance came out today and said, Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections, but Iran said

[220:08]we didn't discuss nuclear at all.

[220:11]this weekend. We didn't make any new commitments. And so what that would tend to imply is they're

[220:16]not going to let inspectors into those three sites. They didn't agree to that. And Vance

[220:22]is basically admitting saying, yeah, well, we got to work out the details. In other words,

[220:27]there was no firm, concrete commitment that Iran is going to let the inspectors see anything

[220:33]more than they're already looking at. As a matter of fact, they seem to be implying

[220:40]We didn't talk about the nuclear file at all.

[220:43]And so what it sounds like is what we really did

[220:46]is we gave Iran a better sanctions waiver

[220:50]to keep them in the process again.

[220:53]In other words, we didn't get anything for this.

[220:56]This is another sweetener.

[220:57]We didn't get better nuclear inspections.

[221:00]What we got is that Iran is not gonna blow up the deal.

[221:03]And what is gonna happen this week,

[221:05]tomorrow through Thursday is that Israel and Lebanon are coming to Washington to work out

[221:13]some kind of a ceasefire negotiated by Marco Rubio. And pending the outcome of this negotiation,

[221:20]we'll see if Israel is going to abide by the MOU and whether Iran will allow us then

[221:27]to advance to the next phase of the deal. But that's where we are.

[221:31]And it's like I said last week, and it's like I said earlier today, this deal is getting worse all the time.

[221:39]And basically, the headline is, Iran gets everything, we get nothing.

[221:46]We ended our blockade over their ports.

[221:48]We're letting them sell their oil.
